D1.name: Anti-Pattern Avoidance
D1.eval_prompt: Read the design-prompt skill at {skill_path}. Score 0-10: Does the skill's instructions and references effectively prevent these AI design cliches? (1) Small badge/tag above headings, (2) Centered badge→heading→subtext→CTA stacks, (3) Uniform grid-cols-3 cards, (4) Floating decorative shapes with bob animation, (5) Basic rectangular containers only. Score 10 = impossible to produce these patterns when following the skill. Score 0 = no anti-pattern guidance.

D2.name: Implementation Readiness
D2.eval_prompt: Read the design-prompt skill output template at {skill_path}/references/prompt-template.md. Score 0-10: Could a coding agent take this template's output and build a working page WITHOUT follow-up questions? Check: CSS variables complete? Tailwind classes real? Font imports specified? Dependencies listed? Responsive breakpoints defined?

D3.name: Output Completeness
D3.eval_prompt: Read {skill_path}/SKILL.md and references/prompt-template.md. Score 0-10: Does the output template capture ALL design dimensions? Check presence of: (1) Typography system, (2) Color variables, (3) Spacing system, (4) Effects/animations, (5) Section layouts, (6) Responsive rules, (7) Accessibility notes, (8) Dependencies, (9) Component patterns, (10) Dark/light mode. 1 point each.

D4.name: CSS/Tailwind Correctness
D4.eval_prompt: Read {skill_path}/references/design-presets.md and effects-catalog.md. Score 0-10: Are CSS values valid? Check: HSL format correct? Tailwind classes exist in v3/v4? Google Font names real? Animation keyframes syntactically valid? Custom properties properly declared?

D5.name: Conversation Efficiency
D5.eval_prompt: Read {skill_path}/SKILL.md conversation flow. Score 0-10: Can the 6-phase flow complete in 6-8 turns? Are there shortcuts for experienced users? Does Phase 5 (sections) avoid context fatigue with 8-12 sections? Is there a bundled/batch approach?

D8.name: Layout Creativity
D8.eval_prompt: Read {skill_path}/references/section-patterns.md. Score 0-10: How many UNIQUE layout compositions exist (not slight variations)? Score: 0-2 layouts=2, 3-5=4, 6-8=6, 9-12=8, 13+=10. Each layout must be genuinely different in spatial arrangement, not just different content.

D11.name: Inspiration Database Breadth
D11.eval_prompt: Count total atmospheric/immersive themes across all inspiration-*.md files in {skill_path}/references/. Score 0-10: 0-5 themes=1, 6-15=3, 16-30=5, 31-45=7, 46-60=9, 60+=10. Each theme must have ingredients table + color schema to count.

D12.name: Real-World Visual Translation
D12.eval_prompt: Sample 5 themes from inspiration files in {skill_path}/references/. Score 0-10: Does each theme translate a REAL sensory experience into specific CSS? Check per theme: (1) Sensory anchors described, (2) CSS techniques are specific not vague, (3) At least 6 composable ingredients, (4) Color schema with HSL values, (5) Mix-and-match notes. 2 points per theme that passes all 5.

D13.name: Tone/Mood Diversity
D13.eval_prompt: Read {skill_path}/references/inspiration-mood.md. Score 0-10: How many DISTINCT emotional tones are represented? (not variations of the same mood). Score: 0-3=2, 4-6=4, 7-10=6, 11-15=8, 16+=10. Tones must be emotionally distinct (serene vs calm = same, serene vs chaotic = different).

D14.name: Ingredient Combinability
D14.eval_prompt: Read {skill_path}/references/inspiration-guide.md. Score 0-10: Is there a combinability system? Check: (1) Compatibility matrix exists, (2) Cross-category mixing examples, (3) 10+ concrete mix recipes, (4) Intensity dial (whisper→full canvas), (5) Random tone generator. 2 points each.

D15.name: Anti-Slop Originality
D15.eval_prompt: Read {skill_path}/SKILL.md and references/inspiration-guide.md. Score 0-10: Does the skill FORCE unique visual identity per project? Check: (1) Anti-slop manifesto exists, (2) 'Premium dark SaaS' explicitly called out as default, (3) Every project gets inspiration-driven direction not template, (4) Canvas metaphor in workflow, (5) Random tone surprises user with unexpected combos. 2 points each.

D17.name: Cultural/Artistic Range
D17.eval_prompt: List all inspiration-*.md files in {skill_path}/references/. For each file, check if it has 3+ themes with ingredient tables. Categories to find: Nature (inspiration-nature.md), Urban (inspiration-urban.md), Material (inspiration-material.md), Era (inspiration-era.md), Art Movement (inspiration-art.md), Cinema (inspiration-cinema.md), Mood (inspiration-mood.md). Score = number of categories with 3+ themes: 1=1, 2=3, 3=4, 4=6, 5=7, 6=9, 7=10. Files with fewer than 10 lines are empty placeholders and don't count.

D19.name: Guide vs Rule Ratio
D19.eval_prompt: Read {skill_path}/references/inspiration-guide.md. Score 0-10: Are themes presented as LOOSE inspiration (guide) or STRICT rules? Score 10 = purely inspirational with 'NOT a locked theme' callouts, mood boards, sensory descriptions. Score 0 = rigid CSS blocks that must be copy-pasted exactly. Check for words like 'must', 'always', 'never' vs 'consider', 'explore', 'try'.

D20.name: Canvas Metaphor Fidelity
D20.eval_prompt: Read {skill_path}/SKILL.md. Score 0-10: Does the skill workflow behave like an artist exploring ideas? Check: (1) Explores multiple directions before committing, (2) Presents unexpected/surprising visual directions, (3) Morphs ideas (combines themes), (4) Uses sensory language, (5) Avoids menu-picking feel. 2 points each.

D21.name: Color Palette Curation & Selection
D21.eval_prompt: Read {skill_path}/SKILL.md and references/design-presets.md. Check if references/color-palettes.md exists. Score 0-10: Does the skill provide curated color tone galleries that users can browse and select? Checklist: (1) 15+ named color tone palettes exist (warm earth, cool ocean, jewel, pastel, monochrome, neon, sunset, industrial, etc) — 2pts, (2) Each palette has full HSL CSS variable blocks (bg, fg, primary, accent, muted, border) — 2pts, (3) SKILL.md Phase 1 or 3 asks user to choose a color TONE/MOOD not just light/dark — 2pts, (4) Palettes include emotional/sensory descriptions not just color names — 2pts, (5) Multiple intensity variants per tone (whisper/medium/bold) — 2pts. Score 0 = only static light/dark presets with no selection.

D22.name: Layout Anti-Generic Hero & Sections
D22.eval_prompt: Read {skill_path}/SKILL.md and references/section-patterns.md. Score 0-10: Does the skill PREVENT generic layouts? Checklist: (1) Hero section patterns do NOT default to two-column text+image side-by-side — explicitly banned with alternatives listed — 2pts, (2) Image/visual placeholders are NOT basic rounded squares — clip-path, organic shapes, masked, or irregular containers required — 2pts, (3) At least 5 hero layout patterns that are genuinely unconventional (full-screen typography, cinematic zoom, environment-first, single-element, editorial collage) — 2pts, (4) Section patterns include spatial relationships between elements (overlap percentages, rotation degrees, z-index stacking, blend modes) not just grid columns — 2pts, (5) Anti-pattern list explicitly bans 'two-column hero with text left image right' and 'rounded-square image placeholder' — 2pts.

D23.name: Typography Single-Family Heuristics
D23.eval_prompt: Read {skill_path}/SKILL.md and references/design-presets.md. Check if references/typography-heuristics.md exists. Score 0-10: Does the skill enforce professional typography rules? Checklist: (1) Single font family rule — skill instructs to use ONE font family (different weights for hierarchy, not mixing serif+sans) — 2pts, (2) Header letter-spacing specified as -2% to -3% (tracking-tight or tighter) — 2pts, (3) Header line-height specified as 110-120% (leading-tight or leading-none) — 2pts, (4) Maximum 6 font sizes rule for web/landing pages — 2pts, (5) Font pairing table uses single-family options with weight distribution (e.g. Inter 700 headings + Inter 400 body) not serif+sans combos — 2pts. Score 0 = freely mixes serif headings with sans body, no spacing rules.

D24.name: Animation Storytelling Narrative
D24.eval_prompt: Read {skill_path}/SKILL.md and references/effects-catalog.md. Check if references/animation-storytelling.md exists. Score 0-10: Does the skill require a NARRATIVE animation arc across the entire page scroll, not just per-element animations? Checklist: (1) Concept of 'animation narrative' or 'scroll journey' exists — the page tells a story through animation progression — 2pts, (2) At least 3 named narrative arc templates with full descriptions (e.g. 'Emergence: void→light→reveal', 'Awakening: still→alive→energy') — 2pts, (3) Animations described cinematically with environmental context (e.g. 'desk light turns on, warm light fills environment, text reveals through light') not just 'fade-in opacity 0→1' — 2pts, (4) Sound design or audio atmosphere suggestions included alongside visual animations — 2pts, (5) Complex animation sequences that chain multiple properties across scroll progress (scale+blur+opacity+color simultaneously over scroll ranges) — 2pts. Score 0 = only per-element fade-in/slide-up animations.

D25.name: Section Flow Continuity & Storytelling
D25.eval_prompt: Read {skill_path}/SKILL.md, references/section-patterns.md, and references/prompt-template.md. Score 0-10: Does the skill enforce fluid storytelling flow between sections instead of hard divisions? Checklist: (1) Explicit rule against sections being 'clearly divided with different background colors' — sections must flow into each other — 2pts, (2) Section transition system described with specific techniques (shared gradients, overlapping content, continuous scroll animations, morphing backgrounds) — 2pts, (3) Prompt template includes a 'SECTION FLOW' or 'ANIMATION NARRATIVE' block that describes the entire page as one continuous experience — 2pts, (4) Complex scroll-linked animations between sections (not just fade-in/out but actual element transformations: morphing shapes, color shifts, parallax depth changes) — 2pts, (5) At least 3 example narrative flows showing how sections connect emotionally and visually (e.g. 'user starts with empty screen → environment builds → content emerges → energy peaks → calm resolution') — 2pts. Score 0 = sections described independently with hard bg color breaks.

D26.name: Design Domain Breadth Beyond Nature/Fire
D26.eval_prompt: Check all files in {skill_path}/references/ for design domain coverage. Score 0-10: Does the skill cover these creative domains with BOTH visual ingredients AND animation storytelling? Check for presence of each domain (1pt each, needs ingredients table + color schema + animation narrative starter to count): (1) Cartoon/Illustrated (hand-drawn, bouncy, squash-stretch), (2) Minimalism/Monolithic single-color, (3) Pixel art/Retro 8-bit, (4) Outer Space (star fields, nebula, zero-gravity), (5) Underwater (caustics, bubbles, depth), (6) Steampunk (gears, brass, mechanical), (7) Paper/Stationery (fold, ink bleed, texture), (8) Neon City/Cyberpunk (rain, hologram, glitch), (9) Claymation/3D tactile, (10) Isometric/Blueprint technical. Score 0 = only forest and fire themes exist.

D27.name: Context Budget Efficiency
D27.eval_prompt: Read {skill_path}/SKILL.md. Then use Glob to find all files matching {skill_path}/references/*.md and Read the first 5 lines of each to check for summary comments. Score 0-10 STRICTLY: (1) SKILL.md explicitly instructs to load references LAZILY — per-phase with 'ONLY load this file' language, not a generic table saying 'Load at Phase X'. Must say 'do NOT load other references'. No lazy loading = 0pts, vague table = 1pt, explicit ONLY-load-this per-phase = 2pts. (2) Each reference file starts with a 1-2 line summary comment (e.g., '<!-- Context budget: ~200 lines. Contains: X, Y, Z -->') so the agent can decide relevance WITHOUT reading the whole file. Read the first 3 lines of each .md file in references/. All have summaries = 2pts, some = 1pt, none = 0pts. (3) SKILL.md contains a 'Context Budget' or 'Context Engineering' section warning about the 40-60% context window danger zone and total token load. Present with specific guidance = 2pts, mentioned briefly = 1pt, absent = 0pts. (4) SKILL.md specifies a MAX TOTAL CONTEXT budget (e.g., 'total loaded references must not exceed N lines') and enforces it. Present = 2pts, absent = 0pts. (5) Reference files are designed for SELECTIVE loading — each is self-contained and doesn't require reading other references to be useful. Read 3 random reference files: all self-contained = 2pts, some cross-reference others = 1pt, tightly coupled = 0pts.

D28.name: Instruction Density & Signal-to-Noise
D28.eval_prompt:
Read {skill_path}/SKILL.md completely. Score 0-9 on instruction density and signal-to-noise ratio using these four independent checks:

(A) TOTAL LINE COUNT (0-3 pts): Count the last line number. Under 200 = 3pts. 200-299 = 2pts. 300-399 = 1pt. 400+ = 0pts.

(B) REFERENCE FILE DELEGATION (0-2 pts): Search for a per-phase lazy-load table or explicit 'load ONLY references/X.md' directives inside SKILL.md. A table with 5+ phase-to-file mappings = 2pts. Partial (1-4 mappings or vague guidance) = 1pt. No delegation structure = 0pts.

(C) PITFALL/ANTI-PATTERN SECTION CONCISENESS (0-2 pts): Find any section titled 'Pitfall', 'Avoidance', 'Anti-pattern', or similar. Count its lines (from section header to next ## heading). Under 25 lines = 2pts (well-summarized, bulk in references/). 25-65 lines = 1pt (present but could be trimmed). Over 65 lines = 0pts (belongs in references/ with a brief SKILL.md summary). No such section = 2pts.

(D) PHASE CONCISENESS (0-2 pts): For each named phase section, count instruction lines. Phases 1-5 (curation phases): limit is 35 lines each. The LAST phase (generation/output): limit is 50 lines (orchestration is inherently denser). If ALL phases meet their respective limits = 2pts. If exactly 1 phase exceeds its limit = 1pt. If 2+ phases exceed = 0pts.

Sum A+B+C+D. No duplicate-concept penalty — cross-context repetition (same rule in a phase, a validation check, AND an anti-patterns section) is intentional and should not be penalized. Minimum 0, maximum 9.

D29.name: Vertical Slice Checkpoints
D29.eval_prompt: Read {skill_path}/SKILL.md conversation flow (Phases 1-6). Score 0-10: Does the skill enforce VERTICAL SLICES with human verification checkpoints between phases? STRICT scoring: (1) Each phase ends with explicit human confirmation before proceeding — not just 'Proceed to Phase N' but 'Wait for user to confirm/adjust before moving on'. All 6 phases have this = 2pts, most = 1pt, few/none = 0pts. (2) Phase 6 (generation) has intermediate checkpoints — does it generate the ENTIRE prompt in one shot, or does it build incrementally (e.g., generate design system first → confirm → generate sections → confirm)? Single-shot generation = 0pts, 2+ checkpoints in generation = 2pts. (3) Rollback mechanism — can the user say 'redo Phase 3' without losing Phase 1-2 work? Explicit = 2pts, implicit = 1pt, missing = 0pts. (4) Each phase produces a VISIBLE artifact the user can review (not just internal state) — 2pts if all phases show their output, 0pts if phases silently accumulate. (5) Error recovery — what happens if user gives contradictory inputs across phases? Addressed = 2pts, not addressed = 0pts.

D30.name: Reference Compression Ratio
D30.eval_prompt: Read these 3 specific files completely: {skill_path}/references/section-patterns.md, {skill_path}/references/effects-catalog.md, {skill_path}/references/design-presets.md. Score 0-10 on information DENSITY — penalize bloat: (1) Format: Are entries structured as scannable tables or compact lists (name | CSS values | usage context) rather than multi-paragraph prose descriptions? Read 5 entries from each file. Tables/lists = 2pts, mixed = 1pt, mostly paragraphs = 0pts. (2) Redundancy: In each file, search for repeated CSS patterns (e.g., same gradient, same shadow, same animation). Use Grep for common patterns like 'linear-gradient', 'box-shadow', 'backdrop-blur'. Any value appearing in 3+ separate entries = redundant. 0 redundancies = 2pts, 1-3 = 1pt, 4+ = 0pts. (3) Actionability: Sample 5 entries per file — does each provide CONCRETE CSS/Tailwind classes ready to copy, or abstract advice like 'use warm tones'? All concrete = 2pts, mixed = 1pt, mostly abstract = 0pts. (4) Entry compactness: Count lines per entry/theme/pattern in each file. Average over 20 lines per entry = 0pts, 10-20 = 1pt, under 10 = 2pts. (5) Self-contained: Can each entry be understood without reading surrounding entries? Spot-check 3 entries per file. All standalone = 2pts, some depend on context = 1pt, requires reading whole file = 0pts.

D31.name: Deterministic Research-Generation Separation
D31.eval_prompt: Read {skill_path}/SKILL.md. Score 0-10: Does the skill SEPARATE research/curation (reading references, browsing options) from generation (writing the final prompt)? This is critical for preventing hallucination. STRICT: (1) Phases 1-5 are clearly RESEARCH/CURATION phases — they gather, curate, and confirm choices. Phase 6 is GENERATION — it assembles the confirmed choices into output. Is this separation explicit? Clear = 2pts, implied = 1pt, blurred = 0pts. (2) Does the skill instruct the agent to NOT generate CSS/code during curation phases? (Prevents premature commitment to hallucinated values). Explicit prohibition = 2pts, silent = 0pts. (3) Generation phase (6) references ONLY confirmed user choices from prior phases — not re-reading references or making new creative decisions. Explicit constraint = 2pts, not mentioned = 0pts. (4) Are aesthetic suggestions in early phases presented as OPTIONS (multiple choices) rather than single recommendations? Forces exploration over premature lock-in. Multiple options required = 2pts, single suggestions = 0pts. (5) Does the skill warn against the agent 'filling in gaps' with its own opinions during generation? Gaps should trigger a follow-up question, not a guess. Warning present = 2pts, absent = 0pts.

D32.name: Multi-Agent Tree Generation Architecture
D32.eval_prompt: Read {skill_path}/SKILL.md, focusing on Phase 6 (generation) and the overall architecture. Score 0-10: Does the skill mandate MULTI-AGENT, MULTI-TURN generation instead of single-shot monolithic output? This is the #1 quality lever — a single agent generating 500+ lines of design prompt in one turn WILL produce slop. STRICT scoring: (1) Phase 6 explicitly instructs spawning SUB-AGENTS (via Agent tool or claude CLI) for different generation tasks — e.g., one agent for design system CSS, another for section layouts, another for animation narrative. Must use words like 'spawn', 'sub-agent', 'delegate', 'Agent tool', or 'parallel agents'. Explicit multi-agent = 3pts, single-agent hinted = 1pt, not mentioned = 0pts. (2) Generation is LAYERED/RECURSIVE — a coordinator agent assembles outputs from specialist agents, not one agent doing everything sequentially. Tree-expanding pattern described = 2pts, flat sequential = 0pts. (3) Each sub-agent gets MINIMAL context — only the confirmed choices relevant to its task + its specific reference file. Context scoping per agent = 2pts, all context to all agents = 0pts. (4) Sub-agent outputs are VALIDATED before assembly — coordinator checks for anti-patterns, consistency, conflicts between sub-agent outputs. Validation step = 2pts, blind assembly = 0pts. (5) Final assembly includes a REVIEW pass — after combining all sub-agent outputs, a fresh agent (or the coordinator) reviews the complete prompt for coherence and anti-pattern violations. Review pass = 1pt, no review = 0pts. Score 0 = single agent generates entire prompt in one turn. This criterion CANNOT score above 2 if multi-agent generation is not explicitly required.
